tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Golden Retrievers are one of the most popular dog breeds known for their friendly nature, intelligence and loyalty.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are highly trainable, energetic and love outdoor activities such as swimming and fetching.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Golden Retrievers are widely used as guide dogs, therapy dogs and search-and-rescue dogs.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: German Shepherds are strong, intelligent and versatile working dogs commonly used in police and military roles.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are loyal, confident and highly trainable, making them excellent guard and service dogs.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: German Shepherds are among the most popular dog breeds for law enforcement agencies.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Siberian Huskies are medium-sized working dogs originally bred for pulling sleds in cold climates.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are energetic, intelligent and known for their striking blue or multicolored eyes.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Siberian Huskies can run long distances and thrive in cold weather environments.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Bulldogs are muscular dogs known for their wrinkled faces and distinctive pushed-in noses.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Despite their tough appearance, Bulldogs are gentle, friendly and affectionate companions.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Bulldog is a symbol of determination and courage in British culture.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Labrador Retrievers are one of the most popular dog breeds in the world known for their friendly and outgoing personality.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are intelligent, energetic and excellent family pets as well as working dogs.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Labradors are commonly used as guide dogs, therapy dogs and rescue dogs.
